BoundingBox.description: Clothing item bounding box in pixel coordinates
BoundingBox.title: BoundingBox

CategoryEnum.description:
11-key product category taxonomy. Aligned with VALID_CATEGORIES in
fashion_domain_research.py.

Maps to both FashionGen and DeepFashion normalised categories via their
respective _CATEGORY_MAP / _DF_CATEGORY_MAP constants.
CategoryEnum.title: CategoryEnum

DatasetSource.description:
Identifies the originating dataset of a fashion item record.

Values are lowercase strings matching the dataset_source fields used
in FashionGenRecord and DeepFashionRecord.

Examples:
    DatasetSource.FASHIONGEN  → "fashiongen"
    DatasetSource.DEEPFASHION → "deepfashion"
DatasetSource.title: DatasetSource

FitEnum.description: 8-value fit taxonomy. Aligned with VALID_FITS in fashion_domain_research.py.
FitEnum.title: FitEnum

GenderEnum.description:
Gender taxonomy key. Aligned with VALID_GENDERS in fashion_domain_research.py.

All dataset-specific gender strings (e.g. "Men", "Women", "Boys") must
be pre-normalised to these keys before populating a UnifiedFashionItem.
GenderEnum.title: GenderEnum

visible.description: True if landmark is visible in the image
visible.title: Visible

OccasionEnum.description: 9-value occasion taxonomy. Aligned with VALID_OCCASIONS in fashion_domain_research.py.
OccasionEnum.title: OccasionEnum

SeasonEnum.description:
5-value season taxonomy. Aligned with VALID_SEASONS in fashion_domain_research.py.

FashionGen infers season from description text (_SEASON_KEYWORDS).
DeepFashion does not annotate season; use ALL_SEASON as default.
SeasonEnum.title: SeasonEnum

StyleEnum.description:
8-value style hierarchy. Aligned with VALID_STYLES in fashion_domain_research.py.

FashionGen infers style from description keywords (_STYLE_KEYWORDS map).
DeepFashion records may leave this field empty (style is not annotated
in DeepFashion annotations).
StyleEnum.title: StyleEnum

description: Canonical fashion item record for the AI Fashion Design Assistant. Stores data from FashionGen and DeepFashion in a unified, taxonomy-aligned format.

example.description: A slim-fit white cotton dress shirt for formal occasions.

image_id.description: Unique identifier for this fashion item across all datasets. Format: '{PREFIX}_{id}' where PREFIX is FG (FashionGen) or DF (DeepFashion). Example: 'FG_0000042', 'DF_img_Blouse_img_00000001'
image_id.title: Image Id

image_path.description: Forward-slash path to the image file, relative to the project root (fashion-ai-assistant/). Example: 'datasets/fashiongen/images/FG_0000042.jpg'
image_path.title: Image Path

source_dataset.description: Originating dataset. Used to apply dataset-specific processing logic during enrichment and training. Values: 'fashiongen' | 'deepfashion'

category.description: Primary product category. One of 11 taxonomy keys: t_shirts, shirts, hoodies, jackets, pants, jeans, shorts, dresses, ethnic_wear, footwear, accessories.

subcategory.description: Optional sub-level category label. Example: 'graphic_tee' (under t_shirts), 'formal_shirt' (under shirts).
subcategory.title: Subcategory

gender.description: Target gender for the clothing item. None is acceptable when the dataset does not annotate gender (e.g. DeepFashion category-level annotations). Values: 'men' | 'women' | 'unisex'

color.description: List of normalised color names for the item. Derived from KB alias → canonical color name mapping. Example: ['Navy', 'White'], ['Olive Green']
color.title: Color

fabric.description: List of normalised fabric names. Example: ['Cotton', 'Polyester'], ['Denim']
fabric.title: Fabric

pattern.description: List of pattern type strings. Example: ['solid'], ['stripes', 'checked']
pattern.title: Pattern

fit.description: Fit type. Values: slim_fit | regular_fit | relaxed_fit | oversized | cropped | skinny | straight | athletic_fit. FashionGen: inferred from description text. DeepFashion: inferred from attribute vector.

style.description: Style hierarchy value. Values: streetwear | luxury | formal | business_casual | techwear | minimalist | vintage | athleisure. FashionGen: inferred from _STYLE_KEYWORDS in description. DeepFashion: typically None (not annotated).

season.description: Season suitability. Values: spring | summer | autumn | winter | all_season. FashionGen: inferred from _SEASON_KEYWORDS in description. DeepFashion: defaults to all_season.

occasion.description: List of suitable occasions. Values: casual | business_casual | formal | party | sport | outdoor | beach | wedding_festive | lounge.
occasion.title: Occasion

description.description: Human-written product description text. FashionGen: extracted from HDF5 'input_description' field. DeepFashion: None (no description annotations).
description.title: Description

attributes.description: Free-form list of attribute label strings. FashionGen: populated from structured attributes dict. DeepFashion: decoded from 1000-dim attribute vector (only +1 = present values included, max 50).
attributes.title: Attributes

landmarks.description: Clothing landmark points. Populated for DeepFashion records. FashionGen records will always have an empty list. Each landmark: name, normalised x/y in [0,1], visibility flag.
landmarks.title: Landmarks

bounding_box.description: Axis-aligned bounding box for the clothing item. DeepFashion: from Anno/list_bbox.txt (pixel + normalised). FashionGen: None (no bbox annotations).

is_valid.description: True when the record passes all hard validation rules. Set automatically by validate_and_report(). Also pre-set by from_fashiongen() / from_deepfashion() based on the source record's is_valid flag.
is_valid.title: Is Valid

errors.description: Hard validation errors from the source pipeline or validate_and_report().
errors.title: Errors

warnings.description: Soft validation warnings. Record is kept but flagged.
warnings.title: Warnings

processed_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when this record was created/unified.
processed_at.title: Processed At

title: UnifiedFashionItem
